title: Honeycomb assembly and packaging system
abstract: A packaging system for honeycomb assemblies, each including a honeycomb body and reinforcing tube held together by an interference fit or axial compression achieved by thermal expansion coefficient differences between the honeycomb body and reinforcing tube. The reinforcing tube then protects the honeycomb body, facilitating a compact and structurally-strong package.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A package of honeycomb assemblies, comprising: a plurality of honeycomb assemblies, wherein each honeycomb assembly comprises: a honeycomb body, wherein the honeycomb body is comprises a ceramic material, wherein the honeycomb body includes flow paths extending lengthwise at least partway therethrough; a reinforcing tube having at least a portion thereof surrounding the honeycomb body, the reinforcing tube having an interior volume defined as all space interior to interior surfaces of the portion of the reinforcing tube surrounding the honeycomb body, wherein, at shipping temperatures of between -50Â° Celsius (C) and 100Â° C, the reinforcing tube provides a radial inward force upon the honeycomb body that results in an interference fit to hold the honeycomb body within the interior volume of the reinforcing tube, whereby the reinforcing tube physically protects the honeycomb body at least to some degree; a package housing containing the plurality of honeycomb assemblies and defining an exterior of the package, the package housing having a package volume defined as all space interior to exterior surfaces of the package housing, wherein the plurality of honeycomb assemblies fill at least 50% of the package volume. 2. The package of claim 1, wherein the honeycomb bodies have a honeycomb volume defined as all space interior to exterior-most surfaces of the respective honeycomb body, and wherein at least 50% of the package volume is filled by the honeycomb volume. 3. The package of claim 2, wherein at least 60% of the package volume is filled by the honeycomb volume. 4. The package of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the honeycomb assemblies are oriented in a vertical direction in the package housing, whereby the reinforcing tubes of the honeycomb assemblies buttress the package housing from crush loading at least to some degree. 5. The package of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the package has a crush strength in the vertical direction of greater than 1000 kPa. 6. A honeycomb assembly, comprising: a honeycomb body, wherein the honeycomb body comprises a ceramic material, and wherein the honeycomb body comprises flow paths extending lengthwise at least partway therethrough; a reinforcing tube having at least a portion thereof surrounding the honeycomb body, wherein the reinforcing tube comprises a metal material, the reinforcing tube having an interior volume defined as all space interior to interior surfaces of the portion of the reinforcing tube surrounding the honeycomb body, wherein, at a room temperature of 25Â° C, the reinforcing tube provides a first radial inward force upon the honeycomb body that results in an interference fit holding the honeycomb body within the interior volume of the reinforcing tube; wherein the metal material of the reinforcing tube has a greater coefficient of thermal expansion than the ceramic material of the honeycomb body such that, when heated to a high temperature, the reinforcing tube provides a second radial inward force upon the honeycomb body that is less than 10% of the first radial inward force, the high temperature being at least 300Â° C and less than a melting temperature of the metal material, whereby heating the honeycomb body to the high temperature aids positioning of the honeycomb body with respect to the reinforcing tube and/or facilitates formation of the interference fit upon cooling of the honeycomb body. 7. The honeycomb assembly of claim 6, wherein the first radial inward force results in at least 1.3 MPa of radial compression on the honeycomb body at the room temperature. 8. The honeycomb assembly of either claim 6 or 7, wherein, at temperatures between 25Â°C to 300Â°C, the metal material has a thermal expansion coefficient of at least 40 Ï 10 " 7 cm/cm/Â°C, ceramic material has a thermal expansion coefficient of no more than 45 x 10 "7 cm/cm/Â°C, and the thermal expansion coefficient of the metal material is greater than that of the ceramic material. 9. The honeycomb assembly of any one of claims 6 to 8, wherein, at the room temperature, the honeycomb body remains held when the honeycomb body is loaded by a push- out force up to 500 newtons distributed over one end of the substrate. 10. The honeycomb assembly of any one of claims 6 to 9, wherein the first radial inward force results in no more than 650 kPa of radial compression on the honeycomb body at the high temperature. 1 1. The honeycomb assembly of claim 10, wherein the high temperature is 500Â° C. 12. The honeycomb assembly of any one of claims 6 to 1 1, wherein the reinforcing tube directly contacts at least some exterior-most surfaces of the honeycomb body. 13. The honeycomb assembly of any one of claims 6 to 12, wherein at least one of: (a) the honeycomb body consists essentially of the ceramic material and (b) the reinforcing tube consists essentially of the metal material. 14. The honeycomb assembly of claim 6, wherein the honeycomb body has end faces and a length therebetween, and the reinforcing tube has ends and a length therebetween; wherein the length of the reinforcing tube is greater than the length of the honeycomb body such that at least one of the ends of the reinforcing tube extends lengthwise beyond one of the end faces of the honeycomb body as the honeycomb body is held within the interior volume of the reinforcing tube, whereby the at least one extending end of the reinforcing tube physically protect to at least some degree the respective end face of the honeycomb body by serving as a raised curb to limit inadvertent end face contact. 15. The honeycomb assembly of claim 14, wherein the honeycomb body is held within the interior volume of the reinforcing tube such that both ends of the reinforcing tube extend lengthwise beyond the respective end faces of the honeycomb body. 16. The honeycomb assembly of claim 15, wherein the ends of the reinforcing tube extend lengthwise beyond the respective end faces of the honeycomb body by at least 5 millimeters at each of the end faces. 17. The honeycomb assembly of any one of claims 6 to 16, wherein the honeycomb body is under axial compression at the room temperature resulting in tension in the portion of the reinforcing tube surrounding the honeycomb body, further holding the honeycomb body within the interior volume of the reinforcing tube. 18. A honeycomb assembly, comprising: a honeycomb body, the honeycomb body having end faces and a length therebetween, wherein the honeycomb body comprises flow paths extending lengthwise at least partway therethrough; a reinforcing tube having at least a portion thereof surrounding the honeycomb body, the reinforcing tube having ends and a length therebetween, and the reinforcing tube having an interior volume defined as all space interior to interior surfaces of the portion of the reinforcing tube surrounding the honeycomb body, wherein, at temperatures of between -50Â° Celsius (C) and 100Â° C, the reinforcing tube provides a radial inward force upon the honeycomb body that results in an interference fit to hold the honeycomb body within the interior volume of the reinforcing tube, wherein the length of the reinforcing tube is greater than the length of the honeycomb body such that at least one of the ends of the reinforcing tube extends lengthwise beyond one of the end faces of the honeycomb body as the honeycomb body is held within the interior volume of the reinforcing tube. 19. The honeycomb assembly of claim 18, wherein the radial inward force results in at least 1.3 MPa of radial compression on the honeycomb body at a room temperature of 25Â° C. 20. The honeycomb assembly of either claim 18 or 19, wherein the honeycomb body comprises a ceramic material and the reinforcing tube comprises a metal material. 21. The honeycomb assembly of claim 20, wherein the ceramic material has a modulus of rupture of at least 0.9 MPa. 22. The honeycomb assembly of either claim 20 or 21, wherein, at temperatures between 25Â°C to 300Â°C, the metal material has a thermal expansion coefficient of at least 40 Ï 10 " 7 cm/cm/Â°C, ceramic material has a thermal expansion coefficient of no more than 45 x 10 "7 cm/cm/Â°C, and the thermal expansion coefficient of the metal material is greater than that of the ceramic material. 23. The honeycomb assembly of any one of claims 18 to 22, wherein the honeycomb assembly is held within the interior volume of the reinforcing tube such that both ends of the reinforcing tube extend lengthwise beyond the respective end faces of the honeycomb body. 24. The honeycomb assembly of claim 23, wherein the ends of the reinforcing tube extend lengthwise beyond the respective end faces of the honeycomb body by at least 5 millimeters at each of the end faces. 25. The honeycomb assembly of any one of claims 18 to 24, wherein, at a room temperature of 25Â° C, the honeycomb body remains held when the honeycomb body is loaded by a push-out force up to 500 newtons distributed over one end of the substrate. 26. The honeycomb assembly of any one of claims 18 to 25, wherein the reinforcing tube directly contacts at least some exterior-most surfaces of the honeycomb body. 27. The honeycomb assembly of any one of claims 18 to 26, wherein the honeycomb body is under axial compression at the room temperature resulting in tension in the portion of the reinforcing tube surrounding the honeycomb body, further holding the honeycomb body within the interior volume of the reinforcing tube. 28. A method of structurally testing a honeycomb body, comprising steps of: positioning the honeycomb body within a tube, wherein, at the time of the positioning, the tube is at a temperature above 200Â° C, wherein the tube has greater coefficient of thermal expansion than the honeycomb body; and then cooling the tube to a temperature of between -50Â° C and 100Â° C, wherein, during the cooling, the tube contracts, applying radial inward force to the honeycomb body, whereby, following the cooling, the ceramic honeycomb body may demonstrate structural integrity by remaining intact or may crack or otherwise fail under the radial load. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the positioning step comprises sub-steps of: tightening the tube around the honeycomb body while the tube is at the temperature above 200Â° C; and then fastening the tube as tightened, while the tube is at the temperature above 200Â° C. 30. The method of either claim 28 or 29, wherein the metal tube directly contacts at least some exterior-most surfaces of the honeycomb body and wherein the radial inward force results in at least 1.3 MPa of compression on the honeycomb body.
